title: verbascum nigrum var. alopecurus
intro: Verbascum nigrum var. alopecurus, also known as the Black Mullein or Woolly Mullein, is a biennial plant native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which make it useful in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis. The health benefits of Verbascum nigrum var. alopecurus include its ability to soothe and calm irritated tissues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in herbal smoking blends due to its expectorant properties.
